columns.description: Hot food must be held at or above the required temperature during storage, display, or transport.

columns.description: Food deemed unfit for human consumption or otherwise is poisonous or a deleterious substances, is diseased, filthy, putrid, or decomposed.

columns.description: Effective control measures are used to eliminate and prevent rodents, flies, roaches and other vermin from the building.  This column only reflects inspection results completed after 11/06/12.

columns.description: Smoking is prohibited inside and eating or drinking establishment.  No Smoking signs must be clearly posted.

columns.description: Toxic substances and pesticides are properly labeled and stored to prevent contamination of food or food preparation surfaces.  This column only reflects inspection results completed after 11/06/12.

columns.description: Potentially hazardous food’s internal temperature is cooled to the proper temperatures within the required time frame.

columns.description: Montgomery County requires that all restaurant chains with 10 or more locations nationally must list on the menu or menu board the calories for each menu item and have available upon request additional written nutritional information.

columns.description: A food handler must not serve or handle food intended for public consumption if known or suspected of having a disease that is transmissible by food.

columns.description: All sewage must be properly disposed in a manner than complies with all applicable approving authorities and remain free from any part of the system overflowing.

columns.description: Employees must wash hands with soap and warm water prior to handling exposed foods or food-contact surfaces, before starting work, as often as required to remove soil and contamination, after using toilet facilities, and before/between glove use.

columns.description: Potentially hazardous food must be cooked to a minimum internal temperature for a required time period.

columns.name: Reheating Time and Temperature (C)
columns.description: Potentially hazardous food must be reheated to a minimum internal temperature within a specified time period and maintained at that temperature for a designated time.

columns.description: Cold food must be held at or below required temperature during storage, display, or transport.

columns.description: Montgomery County prohibits the storage, use or serving of any food item containing 0.5 grams or more of partially hydrogenated vegetable oil, shortening or margarine.  Does not apply to any product sold in the manufacture’s original sealed packaging.

columns.name: Food from Approved Source (C)
columns.description: All food, or food ingredients, must derive from a source regulated by an approving authority (private homes or unlicensed establishments are not approved sources) and are not adulterated or misbranded.

name: Central Processing Unit (CPU) Processing/Booking of Arrestees

columns.name: Released Unsecured Bond
columns.description: A defendant is released from Central Processing by a District Court Commissioner. An initial cash bond amount is set but does not need to be paid before release. If the defendant fails to appear for Court of violates any pre-trial conditions that are set by a Judge or Commissioner, the cash bond then becomes payable.

columns.name: Circuit Court Warrant
columns.description: The warrant is usually issued for a failure to appear (FTA) for a court date but can be issued for violating pre-trial conditions or violating probation, among other things. Circuit Court warrants are associated with Circuit Court cases. Generally, Circuit Court cases deal with felonies or misdemeanors where a Jury trial is requested.

columns.name: Committed with Bond
columns.description: If a District Court Judge or Commissioner determines that a defendant has a history of failing to appear for court or defendant is a threat to the community or themselves, a cash bond will be set and needs to be paid before the defendant can be released from central processing. The defendant must still appear for their court date.

columns.name: District Court Arrest Warrant
columns.description: An application for an arrest warrant can be filed by Law Enforcement or the general public. A District Court Commissioner reads the application to determine if there is probable cause or enough evidence to arrest the person in question. If probable cause is met, a warrant is issued for the arrest of said person.

columns.name: Traffic
columns.description: Count of individuals booked in CPU for traffic related offenses

columns.name: On View Arrest (SOC)
columns.description: A warrantless arrest made by law enforcement officers at the approximate time of the commission of a crime. The defendant is remanded to the central processing unit, while the arresting officer prepares the charging documents. The defendant has an initial hearing with a District Court Commissioner to determine the conditions of release (if release is appropriate).

columns.name: District Court Bench Warrant
columns.description: A warrant issued by a District Court Judge. The warrant is usually issued for a failure to appear (FTA) for a Court date but can be issued for violating Pre-trial conditions or violating probation, among other things. District Court Bench warrants are associated with District Court cases . Generally District Court cases deal with misdemeanors where the defendant does not request Jury trial.

columns.name: Criminal
columns.description: Count of individuals booked in CPU for crime related offenses. [Shift 1 + Shift 2 + Shift 3] - [total Traffic offenses]

columns.name: Released Personal Recognizance
columns.description: A defendant is released from Central Processing by a District  Court Commissioner. There is no cash bond to be paid and no pre-trial conditions placed on the defendant. Defendant must appear for his/her trial date.

name: Real Property Tax Rates

columns.name: TOTAL SPECIAL SERVICE AREA TAX
columns.description: The Special Service Area Tax includes Transit, Fire District, Advanced Land Acquisition, Metropolitan, Regional, Recreation, Storm Drainage, Parking Lot District, Urban District, Noise Abatement District, and Development District taxes.

columns.name: DEVELOPMENT DISTRICT TAX
columns.description: Development District and Special Taxing District Tax is levied to finance certain infrastructure improvements through secured bond issuance.  Revenues collected are used exclusively for the applicable district.

columns.name: METRO-POLITAN TAX
columns.description: Metropolitan Tax funds M-NCPPC local park facilities and parks programs.  Whether a taxpayer pays one of these special area taxes depends on where the property is located.

columns.name: MUNICIPAL DISTRICT TAX
columns.description: Municipal District Property Tax is levied by each municipal area and generally funds street and sidewalk maintenance, trash removal, tree care, sanitation, and police protection.

columns.name: GENERAL COUNTY TAX
columns.description: The General County tax is levied on all property in the County and funds, in part, such basic services as police protection, elementary and secondary education, the community college, transportation, health and social services, and libraries.

columns.name: ADVANCED LAND ACQUISITION TAX
columns.description: Advance Land Acquisition Tax is levied Countywide and funds land acquisitions by the Maryland-National Capital Park and Planning Commission (M-NCPPC).

columns.name: STORM DRAINAGE TAX
columns.description: Storm Drainage Tax funds storm drainage improvements.  Whether a taxpayer pays one of these special area taxes depends on where the property is located.

columns.name: REGIONAL TAX
columns.description: Regional Tax funds M-NCPPC planning and administrative programs.  Whether a taxpayer pays one of these special area taxes depends on where the property is located.

columns.name: TRANSIT TAX
columns.description: Transit Tax is levied Countywide and funds public transportation services including the Ride On bus system.

columns.name: STATE TAX
columns.description: This tax is levied by the State of Maryland for the payment of principal and interest on State bonds.

columns.name: RECREATION TAX
columns.description: Recreation tax funds recreation facilities and programs.  Whether a taxpayer pays one of these special area taxes depends on where the property is located.

columns.name: PARKING LOT DISTRICT TAX
columns.description: Parking Lot District Tax funds parking lot districts improvements.  Whether a taxpayer pays one of these special area taxes depends on where the property is located.

columns.name: URBAN DISTRICT TAX
columns.description: Urban District Tax funds urban district improvements.  Whether a taxpayer pays one of these special area taxes depends on where the property is located.

columns.name: NOISE ABATEMENT DISTRICT TAX
columns.description: Noise Abatement Tax funds noise abatement district improvements.  Whether a taxpayer pays one of these special area taxes depends on where the property is located.

columns.name: FIRE DISTRICT TAX
columns.description: Fire District Tax is levied Countywide and funds fire and rescue services.

description: The Levy Year 2012 real property tax rate dataset reflects all the rates per $100 set each year by the County Council. name: DEP - Levy Year 2024 Solid Waste Charges

columns.name: Disposal Charge
columns.description: Fee for the delivery and disposal of solid waste to the Shady Grove Transfer Station. Disposal Charge = Base Charge * Billing Rate

columns.name: Leaf Vacuuming Charge
columns.description: Leaf Vacuuming Charges are assessed to owners of residences in the Leaf Vacuuming Collection District to cover the cost of specific leaf vacuuming services provided in the Fall by the Department of Transportation.

columns.name: Sub category
columns.description: Sub categories provide further details about solid waste generators: residences in unincorporated areas or within an incorporated municipality; within Leaf Vacuuming districts or not; Nonresidential generators waste generation categories.

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Types of solid waste generators, such as single-famil and multi-family residences and non-residential generators. *Note: Base Systems Benefit Charges are set to cover County Base Systems Costs net of Disposal Charges; **  With respect to Base and Incremental Systems Benefit Charges, this category includes dwellings in buildings of six or fewer households; ***  The Nonresidential rate multiplied by the total number of 2,000 square foot units of enclosed area equals the nonresidential charge.

columns.name: Total Bill
columns.description: Sum of appropriate charges for each type of solid waste generator. Total Bill =Disposal Charge + Base Systems Benefit Charge + Incremental Systems Benefit Charge + Refuse Collection Charge + Leaf Vacuuming Charge

columns.name: Incremental Systems Benefit Charge
columns.description: Incremental Systems Benefit Charges are assessed to each generating sector for services not equally available or provided to all sectors equally. For example, the County's curbside recycling collection is provided only to single-family homes outside municipal areas; only homes which receive the service are charged to cover its cost. Multi-family and non-residential sectors are charged for recycling outreach and other activities oriented to those waste generating sectors.

columns.name: Base Systems Benefit Charge
columns.description: A solid waste service charge imposed on owners of all real property, both residential and nonresidential, as generators of solid waste.

columns.name: Billing Rate
columns.description: Projected amount of disposable waste generated per household within the county refuse collection districts.

columns.name: Base charge
columns.description: Base Systems Benefit Charges are assessed to the owners of each "improved" property in the County (e.g. occupancy permitted residences and non-residential improvement with assessed value > $5,000).

columns.name: Refuse Collection Charge
columns.description: Refuse Collection Charges are assessed to households in the Collection District that receive refuse collection by County-contracted collectors. These are single-family households or dwellings in buildings comprised of six or fewer dwelling units located in the District. Municipalities are not included.
